events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only, no prose. Need interpret schedule. Let's parse scenario.

Image schedule early-start bars. Activities A through Q. Each with durations and predecessors.

A Mobilize & layout [3d] start
B Underground rough-in [4d] <- A
C Slab pour [2d] <- B
CC Slab cure [3d] <- C (hatched)
D Framing [5d] <- CC
E Roof dry-in [2d] <- D
F Electrical rough [3d] <- E
G Plumbing top-out [3d] <- E
H HVAC rough [2d] <- E
I Rough inspection [1d] <- F,G,H
J Insulation [2d] <- I
K Insulation inspection [1d] <- J
L Drywall hang [5d] <- K
M Tape & finish [4d] <- L
N Paint [2d] <- M
O Trim carpentry [5d] <- N
P Flooring [4d] <- N
Q Final inspection [1d] <- O,P

Disruption: Activity H (HVAC rough) is ON HOLD indefinitely as of day 19 (materials unavailable). Activities finishing by day 19 are complete; nothing else has started.

We need list activity IDs crews can start now: not blocked by the held activity directly or transitively, with all other predecessors complete.

Held H on day 19. Activities finishing by day 19 complete. Need determine current date? As of day 19, activities whose bars finish by day 19 complete. Since H is on hold as of day 19. Activities finishing by day 19 likely A,B,C,CC,D,E,F,G? Let's verify early start bars.

A: 3 days -> finish day 3.
B: 4d start after A finish? bars from day3 to 7.
C: start day7 finish day9.
CC: start day9 finish day12 (hatched).
D: start day12 finish day17.
E: start day17 finish day19.
F: start day19? Wait F Electrical rough duration 3d, starts at day19 finish day22? G and H also start day19 finish day21/20. So F, G, H start day19, not finish by day19. The instruction says "Activities finishing by day 19 are complete; nothing else has started." As of day 19, F, G, H start day 19 but not finish by day 19, so not complete. Actually nothing else has started means any activity not finishing by day 19 has not started. But F, G, H start at day 19. Are they considered started? Probably not because finish after day 19. So they are not complete and have not started. H is on hold. F and G have predecessors E complete, so they could start now if not held/blocked. But note all three F, G, H are successors of E and start at day 19. They are not started. We can start F and G because all predecessors complete (E complete). H cannot start because held. Any activity downstream of H (I) requires H; blocked transitively. I cannot start, J cannot, etc.

Question: "List, in the `workable` field, the IDs of every activity the crews can start now: not blocked by the held activity directly or transitively, with all other predecessors complete." So start now means at current day 19, with all predecessors complete. Need include activities whose predecessors all complete and not blocked transitively. Let's list.

Complete by day19: A, B, C, CC, D, E. Possibly also F/G/H? No. H on hold means not complete, blocked. F and G not complete, but can start.

Activities:
A complete.
B complete.
C complete.
CC complete.
D complete.
E complete.
F: pred E complete, not held, not blocked -> start.
G: pred E complete -> start.
H: held -> cannot.
I: pred F,G,H; H not done -> blocked (transitively by H) -> cannot.
J: pred I -> blocked.
K: pred J -> blocked.
L: pred K -> blocked.
M: pred L -> blocked.
N: pred M -> blocked.
O: pred N -> blocked.
P: pred N -> blocked.
Q: pred O,P -> blocked.

What about activities independent of H but with other predecessors? Only F and G. Also maybe there are other trades not shown? No.

Need provide JSON with all fields. decision: pass? The work can proceed partially. The schedule disruption but not dangerous/non-compliant. decision probably "needs_more_info"? The task says decide what work can proceed, what is blocked, and what recovery plan is valid. The schedule shows a hold. Since we can answer, maybe decision = "pass"? In these benchmarks, decision pass/fail/needs_more_info about work condition. Since hold is schedule, not dangerous/non-compliant. If the work is dangerous or non-compliant,…
